description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly grayish-brown surface, featuring patches of lighter and darker hues. One side of the stone displays a prominent white mineral inclusion, while another side has a dark circular mark, possibly from natural mineral deposits. The surface is rough and unpolished, with visible granular texture and minor indentations. On one face, the word 'Fountain' is written in blue marker, accompanied by a small blue circle, suggesting it may have been used as a marker or sign in an urban setting. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications beyond the writing.
description: A rough, gray-brown stone with a white mineral patch and blue writing on one face, showing natural texture and minor surface marks.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's irregular shape, rough texture, and a visible white mineral patch on the lower right side.
description_view_1: This side shows a darker, more uniform brown surface with a distinct dark circular spot and less visible mineral inclusions.
description_view_2: The white mineral inclusion is most prominent here, set within the rough brown matrix, and the stone's uneven surface is clearly visible.
description_view_3: The face with the blue 'Fountain' label and circle is shown, with a generally flat surface and subtle color variations.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with a large white mineral patch and a dark circular spot; blue handwritten text and circle on one face.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with minor surface pitting, indicating moderate natural weathering and exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Likely to contrast with paved surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; blue writing makes it stand out as a marker.
